title: IDOR Vulnerability
description: The lack of information on the system’s authorization mechanisms creates a potential for IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) vulnerabilities. Attackers could manipulate request parameters to access resources or perform actions that they should not be authorized to perform, potentially gaining elevated privileges.
attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in the application's authorization logic, manipulating request parameters to access unauthorized resources.
mitigations: Implement robust authorization controls based on the principle of least privilege.
Avoid using direct object references (e.g., database IDs) in URLs or request parameters.
Use indirect object references or access control lists (ACLs) to control access to resources.
